title: A Hazmat Employee Must Receive The Required Usdot
source_filename: A Hazmat Employee Must Receive The Required Usdot.docx

qas.q: Hazmat employee training shall include:
qas.a:
General awareness/familiarization training
Function-specific training
Safety training

qas.q: General Awareness Training
qas.a: each hazmat employee is aware of DOT regulations pertaining to hazardous materials.

qas.q: Familiarization Training
qas.a: more in-depth in that Hazmat employees know where to locate DOT hazmat regulations and are able to recognize and identify DOT defined hazardous materials.

qas.q: Function-Specific Training
qas.a: training and testing of hazmat employees who are working with hazardous materials that will be transported in commerce. This means each Hazmat employee must be trained on procedures, specifics, and functions that directly effect their jobs. Should be limited to just the individual employee's hazmat functions, which may encompass only one or two specific duties.

qas.q: Safety Training
qas.a: all hazmat employees must have a working knowledge of emergency response information, which means knowing who to call in the event of an accident. Specifically, DOT's North American Emergency Response Guidebook (NAERG) is used as a text for this training. DOT permits OSHA or EPA training to satisfy this requirement to the extent that the training includes DOT specified instructional topics.

qas.q: A new hazmat employee, or a hazmat employee who changes job functions may perform those functions prior to the completion of the training provided that:
qas.a:
The employee performs those functions under the direct supervision of a properly trained and knowledgeable hazmat employee and,
The training is completed within 90 days after employment or a change in job function.

qas.q: Hazmat employees must be trained and tested on applicable areas of General Awareness/Familiarization, Function-Specific and Safety training with what frequency?
A record of current training, inclusive of the preceding three years, must be created and retained by each hazmat employer for how long?
qas.a: as long as that employee is employed by that employer as a hazmat employee and for 90 days thereafter.

qas.q: Training record must include:
qas.a:
the Hazmat employee's name
the most recent training completion date of the hazmat employee's training
a description, copy, or the location of the training materials used to meet this requirement
the name and address of the person providing the training
A certificate issued that states the hazmat employee has been trained and tested in accordance with 49 CFR 172.700

qas.q: A person who knowingly violates a requirement of the Federal hazardous material transportation law is liable for a civil penalty of:
Identify if employee is a hazmat employee: Traffic manager who makes classification decision
Identify if employee is a hazmat employee: Packaging clerk, readied the shipment
Identify if employee is a hazmat employee: Material handler, takes the package to the dock
Identify if employee is a hazmat employee: Secretary, types up the shipping paper
Identify if employee is a hazmat employee: Quality Assurance Specialist, puts a security seal tag on the lock
Identify if employee is a hazmat employee: Buyer, writes specifications for purchase of boxes, cylinders, etc.
Identify if employee is a hazmat employee: Distribution clerk, transports from receiving dock to a building on site.
qas.a: No. Hazardous material is being moved on-site, so DOT regulations do not apply.

The maximum fine an employer can receive for not training its employees on all topics required by DOT is
A person who is involved in the packaging and transportation of hazardous materials in commerce is defined by DOT to be a:
DOT's hazardous materials training requirement is for
If hazardous materials are moved only on-site,:
qas.a: workers need not be trained on DOT hazardous materials regulations.
